purpose: Support download assets, retention flows and homeowner handover/certificate logic with current official-source framing.

claims_for_pack: Building regulations approval is different from planning permission and a project might need both.
Homeowners must check whether they need approval before constructing or changing buildings in relevant ways.
If a person cannot decide whether approval is needed, GOV.UK says to check with a building control body.
For work that is not higher risk, approval may be from a local authority building control department or a private registered building control approver.
Without approval, certificates of compliance may be missing when the owner wants to sell the home.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: Certificate trackers, evidence folders, before-you-start packs and cross-site planning-vs-building-regs prompts.

claims_for_pack: Competent person schemes allow registered tradespeople to prove they can carry out certain work to required standards instead of the homeowner applying for building regulations approval.
Registered installers can self-certify compliance, notify the local authority if needed, and provide a certificate within 8 weeks of completion.
GOV.UK points users to the Competent Persons Register and Electrical Competent Person Register.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: Competent-person certificate tracker and installer confirmation forms.

claims_for_pack: Approved Documents give guidance on ways to meet the building regulations.
They include general guidance on expected performance and practical examples/solutions for common building situations.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: Approved Document pack downloads and source/version panels.

claims_for_pack: Approved Document L 2026 was published on 24 March 2026 and applies to England.
Earlier versions of Approved Document L continue to apply to buildings subject to previous regulatory standards.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: Version warning for energy/ventilation downloads and saved project packs.

claims_for_pack: The 2026 circular announced new statutory guidance in Approved Document L Volumes 1 and 2 and Approved Document F Volume 1.

It includes new or updated guidance on heat networks, low carbon heating systems, building services efficiencies, commissioning and the role of competent person schemes for heat pump or mechanical ventilation installations.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: Keep energy, heat-pump and ventilation lead magnets version-aware.

claims_for_pack: BSR is the building control authority for higher-risk buildings in England.
Higher-risk buildings are at least 7 storeys or at least 18 metres high and have 2 or more residential units, or are a hospital or care home.
observed_source_claims.use_in_batch_4: HRB exclusion/routing warnings in checklists and tool results.
